rationale: For a Romanian resident, falling oil prices from the US-Iran deal announcement provide mild cost-of-living relief, while the Ukraine war near Romania's border continues without new escalation.

top_events.title: Oil prices slide after US-Iran deal announced
top_events.summary: Lower oil prices ease energy and fuel costs for Romanian households — continuation of last cycle's trend with no new qualitative development.

top_events.title: Russian strikes kill nine in Ukraine and damage historic cathedral
top_events.summary: Ongoing Russian strikes in Ukraine remain background noise for Romania — no new escalation toward the Black Sea or Romanian border this cycle.

top_events.title: US-Iran to sign a 'peace deal' on Friday
top_events.summary: A formal peace deal signing would further stabilize oil markets and reduce regional risk — still rhetoric/announcement stage, no signed outcome yet.
